concerns.analysis: The extraction model consistently stripped inline hyperlinks from body paragraphs and appended them as disconnected links at the bottom of the pages. Because the links are missing from the actual anchor text in the reading flow, they are functionally missing from their context.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The birds listed below are birds of particular concern either because they occur on the USFWS Birds of Conservation Concern (BCC) list or warrant special attention...
concerns.output_quote: "The birds listed below are birds of particular concern either because they occur on the USFWS Birds of Conservation Concern (BCC) list or warrant special attention in your project location."

concerns.analysis: Several full-page map diagrams that are critical to understanding the site conditions are completely missing from the HTML output. Specifically, the 'Soil Contaminant Concentration Map' on page 18, the 'Groundwater Contaminant Concentration Map' on page 19, and the 'Sub-Slab Gas and Indoor Air Contaminant Concentration Map' on page 20 have not been rendered as images in the output. The HTML only contains the extracted text elements (legends, notes, and title blocks) and placeholders for small ic
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 18 displays a full-page diagram titled 'Soil Contaminant Concentration Map' showing property boundaries, a building footprint, and contour lines for PCE and TCE.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML contains text extracted from the map (such as the NOTES and the TITLE block) and text placeholders like '[Image: North arrow and graphic scale bar (0 to 40 feet)]', but there is no `` element corresponding to the main map graphic itself.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (109): i; ii; https://harthick.sharepoint.com/sites/MasterFiles-1/Shared D
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=109, pages_affected=51/73. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

The source document contains a two-column table spanning pages 44 and 45. The header row on page 44 ('NAME' and 'BREEDING SEASON...') is extracted as a single-row table. However, the data rows corresponding to these columns on page 45 (e.g., 'Bald Eagle Haliaeetus leucocephalus' and 'Breeds Sep 1 to Jul 31') are completely stripped of their tabular structure and extracted as independent headings and paragraphs. This destroys the structural relationship between the data and the columns.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Two-column table spanning pages 44-45 with headers 'NAME' and 'BREEDING SEASON...' and data rows like 'Bald Eagle Haliaeetus leucocephalus' | 'Breeds Sep 1 to Jul 31'.
concerns.output_quote: Page 44 contains a 1-row table for the headers. Page 45 extracts the tabular data as flat text: {"type": "heading", "level": 3, "text": "Bald Eagle Haliaeetus leucocephalus"} followed by {"type": "paragraph", "text": "Breeds Sep 1 to Jul 31"}.

concerns.analysis: On page 28, there is a continuous numbered list (1 through 4) with paragraph answers appearing below each question. The output extracts each numbered question as a separate, newly instantiated ordered list containing only one item. This causes the HTML rendering to restart the numbering at '1' for every question, rather than maintaining the continuous 1-4 sequence present in the source.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Continuous numbered list: '1. Are there navigable water bodies...', '2. Are there any water bodies...', '3. Are there any wetland...', '4. Are there any sensitive...'
concerns.output_quote: Each question is extracted as an independent list block: {"type": "list", "list_type": "ordered", "items": [{"text": "Are there navigable..."}]} followed by a paragraph, then a new {"type": "list", "list_type": "ordered", "items": [{"text": "Are there any water bodies..."}]}.
concerns.location: Page 28 (Level 1 Ecological Risk Assessment Checklist A)

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 56 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=56, examples=['https://harthick.sharepoint.com/sites/MasterFiles-1/Shared D', 'http://nwi.fws.gov/', 'https://fws.maps.arcgis.com/home/webmap/print.html']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: 3 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://www.fws.gov/birds/management/managed-species/birds-of-conservation-concer; http://www.fws.gov/birds/management/project-assessment-tools-and-guidance/conser; http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/wm/DSCA/PublicNotices.
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=3. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 10 form(s) with 25 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=10, total_fields=25. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "if you have any questions, please feel free to contact me at"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 17 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=17. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
